name: pages
context: Deploy static sites and full-stack applications to Cloudflare Pages with Git integration and edge functions
info: Cloudflare Pages deploys static sites and full-stack applications to the edge. It integrates with GitHub and GitLab for automatic deployments on push. Pages supports all major frameworks (Next.js, Astro, SvelteKit, Nuxt, Remix, Vite) via build presets. Pages Functions provide server-side compute using the Workers runtime, enabling full-stack apps with file-based routing in the `functions/` directory. Preview deployments are automatic for every branch and PR. Custom domains, redirects, headers, and environment variables are configurable. Free tier: unlimited sites, unlimited requests, unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month.
triggers: deploy a site to cloudflare pages
deploy a static site to cloudflare
deploy a fullstack app to cloudflare
body:
# Cloudflare Pages

Pages deploys static and full-stack applications to the edge with automatic Git integration, preview deployments, and zero configuration for popular frameworks.

## Quick Start

### From Git (Dashboard)

1. Go to Cloudflare Dashboard > Workers & Pages > Create
2. Connect GitHub/GitLab account
3. Select repository
4. Configure build settings (framework preset auto-detected)
5. Deploy

### From CLI

```bash
# Deploy a directory directly
wrangler pages deploy ./dist

# Deploy with project name
wrangler pages deploy ./dist --project-name my-site

# Create a new Pages project
wrangler pages project create my-site --production-branch main
```

## Framework Presets

Pages auto-detects and configures builds for:

| Framework | Build Command | Output Directory |
|-----------|---------------|------------------|
| Next.js | `npx @cloudflare/next-on-pages` | `.vercel/output/static` |
| Astro | `npm run build` | `dist` |
| SvelteKit | `npm run build` | `.svelte-kit/cloudflare` |
| Nuxt | `npm run build` | `.output/public` |
| Remix | `npm run build` | `build/client` |
| Vite/React | `npm run build` | `dist` |
| Hugo | `hugo` | `public` |
| Gatsby | `gatsby build` | `public` |

## Pages Functions (Full-Stack)

Add server-side logic with file-based routing in the `functions/` directory:

```typescript
// functions/api/hello.ts
export const onRequestGet: PagesFunction = async (context) => {
  return Response.json({ message: 'Hello from Pages Functions!' });
};

export const onRequestPost: PagesFunction<Env> = async (context) => {
  const body = await context.request.json();
  const result = await context.env.DB.prepare(
    'INSERT INTO items (name) VALUES (?) RETURNING id'
  ).bind(body.name).first();
  return Response.json(result, { status: 201 });
};
```

```
functions/
├── api/
│   ├── hello.ts          → GET/POST /api/hello
│   ├── users/
│   │   ├── index.ts      → GET/POST /api/users
│   │   └── [id].ts       → GET/PUT/DELETE /api/users/:id
│   └── [[path]].ts       → Catch-all /api/*
└── _middleware.ts         → Runs before all functions
```

## Custom Domains

```bash
# Add a custom domain
wrangler pages project create my-site
# Then in Dashboard: Pages project > Custom domains > Set up a domain

# Or via API
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/$ACCOUNT_ID/pages/projects/my-site/domains" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{"name":"app.example.com"}'
```

## Environment Variables

```bash
# Set environment variables for Pages
wrangler pages secret put DATABASE_URL --project-name my-site

# Or in wrangler.toml for Pages
# [vars]
# API_URL = "https://api.example.com"
```

## Preview Deployments

Every push to a non-production branch creates a preview deployment at:
`https://<commit-hash>.my-site.pages.dev`

PR comments show the preview URL automatically when GitHub integration is configured.

## Redirects and Headers

```
# _redirects file in output directory
/old-page /new-page 301
/blog/* /news/:splat 302
```

```
# _headers file in output directory
/api/*
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
  Cache-Control: no-cache

/*.js
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable
```

## Bindings in Pages

Pages Functions support the same bindings as Workers:

```toml
# wrangler.toml
[[d1_databases]]
binding = "DB"
database_name = "my-db"
database_id = "xxx"

[[kv_namespaces]]
binding = "KV"
id = "yyy"

[[r2_buckets]]
binding = "BUCKET"
bucket_name = "my-bucket"
```

sections.title: Deploying Pages
sections.body: Deploy via `wrangler pages deploy ./dist` for direct uploads, or connect a Git repo through the dashboard for automatic deploys on push. Use `--project-name` to specify the project. Framework presets auto-detect build commands and output directories. Production deploys go to `<project>.pages.dev`; branch deploys go to `<branch>.<project>.pages.dev`.

sections.title: Pages Functions
sections.body: Add server-side routes by creating files in the `functions/` directory. File-based routing: `functions/api/users.ts` maps to `/api/users`. Dynamic routes use `[param].ts` brackets. Export `onRequestGet`, `onRequestPost`, etc. for method-specific handlers. `_middleware.ts` runs before all functions in its directory. Functions use the Workers runtime and support all Worker bindings.

sections.title: Preview deployments
sections.body: Every push to a non-production branch creates a unique preview URL: `https://<hash>.<project>.pages.dev`. Branch-specific previews use: `https://<branch>.<project>.pages.dev`. GitHub integration adds preview URLs to PR comments. Preview deployments use preview bindings (KV, D1, R2) from `wrangler.toml`.

sections.title: Framework support
sections.body: Pages supports all major frameworks: Next.js (via @cloudflare/next-on-pages), Astro, SvelteKit, Nuxt, Remix, Vite, Hugo, Gatsby. Build presets auto-configure the build command and output directory. Next.js on Pages requires `@cloudflare/next-on-pages` adapter. Astro and SvelteKit have native Cloudflare adapters.
